search_evidence: Rear ARB size was searched as part of the BMW/Sebring rotation-control stage.
Rear ARB blade was searched with long-exit corner weighting instead of staying at the baseline blade.
Front camber was searched using loaded-front support, exit push, and tyre thermal evidence.
Rear camber was searched from traction demand, body-slip stability, and rear tyre support.
Front toe was searched from low-speed push, yaw correlation, and front thermal support.
Rear toe was searched for the traction and rotation compromise instead of staying stability-only.
Preload was searched against exit-push, instability, and rear power-slip evidence.
The legal coupled ramp option was searched with extra weight on Sebring long-exit understeer.
Clutch plate count was searched both toward less lock and more lock based on exit and traction evidence.

solver_notes: Materialized candidate through shared solve chain.
Applied family-specific supporting overrides on top of fresh supporting solve.
BMW/Sebring rotation search scored 378 abstract candidates.
Telemetry rotation state: entry_push=0.06; exit_push=3.10; instability=0.20; traction_risk=0.31; long_exit_bias=4.28.
Base rotation score 20.659: pred_exit=3.10; pred_entry=0.06; pred_instability=0.20; pred_traction=0.31.

Selected rotation profile 'free_exit_rotation' at 15.221: diff 20Nm / 50/75 / 6 plates; toe F-0.6 R+0.2; camber F-2.4 R-1.7; RARB Medium/2 | pred_exit=0.84; pred_entry=0.00; pred_instability=1.08; pred_traction=1.06
Materialized 12 BMW/Sebring rotation candidates and selected the lowest-penalty combination.
Applied rematerialized compromise candidate result to final report/JSON/export payloads.
Preserved BMW/Sebring second-stage rotation controls after candidate-family rematerialization.

constraints.name: Torsion bar OD valid (14.34mm)
constraints.detail: OD 14.34mm not in discrete garage options

constraints.name: RARB sensitivity within useful range
constraints.note: <0.005 = insensitive (wrong bar size), >0.05 = too sensitive (step down)

car_specific_notes: Explicit ARB materialization preserves the selected bar/blade family and recomputes LLTD.
Front ARB Soft/1, rear ARB Medium/2.

rear_arb_size: Rear ARB size was searched as part of the BMW/Sebring rotation-control stage.
rear_arb_blade: Rear ARB blade was searched with long-exit corner weighting instead of staying at the baseline blade.

constraints.name: Front dynamic camber at peak g
Rear dynamic camber at peak g
Rear toe non-destabilizing
constraints.note: Rear toe-out is destabilizing — keep at or above 0mm

notes: Explicit geometry materialization at representative roll 2.3°.

front_toe_mm: Front toe was searched from low-speed push, yaw correlation, and front thermal support.
rear_toe_mm: Rear toe was searched for the traction and rotation compromise instead of staying stability-only.
front_camber_deg: Front camber was searched using loaded-front support, exit push, and tyre thermal evidence.
rear_camber_deg: Rear camber was searched from traction demand, body-slip stability, and rear tyre support.

step6_dampers.hs_slope_reasoning: Front p99/p95=1.99 -> slope 11, Rear p99/p95=1.89 -> slope 11

constraints.name: Front LS damping ratio
constraints.note: 0.3-1.0 valid range for racing. GTP front uses high ζ due to soft springs.

constraints.name: Rear HS damping ratio
constraints.note: Must be low for rear traction over bumps. >0.4 = snap oversteer risk.

constraints.name: Front HS rbd > HS comp
constraints.note: Rebound must exceed comp to prevent wheel bounce.

constraints.name: Rear HS comp < Front HS comp
constraints.note: Compliance hierarchy: rear yields to bumps more than front.

constraints.name: Front LS comp >= Rear LS comp
constraints.note: Front controls entry. If violated: car will be nervous on entry.

constraints.name: Rear shock oscillation vs natural freq
constraints.note: Ratio: 2.48x natural freq (3.51 Hz). >1.5x = underdamped evidence. ζ_hs_rear bumped to 0.203.

notes: Front critical damping: 7380 N*s/m (modal k=49.3 N/mm, f_n = 2.13 Hz)
Rear critical damping: 13598 N*s/m (modal k=150.1 N/mm, f_n = 3.51 Hz)
Front LS: zeta=0.85 -> c=6273 N*s/m -> F@25mm/s = 157 N -> 9 clicks
Front HS: zeta=0.43 -> c=3208 N*s/m -> F@126mm/s = 404 N -> 5 clicks
Rear HS: zeta=0.20 -> c=2758 N*s/m -> F@167mm/s = 461 N -> 6 clicks
HS ref velocities: front p95=126.0mm/s, rear p95=167.2mm/s (rear +33% more active)
Damping ratios are derived from quarter-car eigenvalue analysis, NOT from empirical baseline matching.

supporting.brake_bias_reasoning: Calibrated base: 46.0% | Fuel correction: -0.01% at 20L | Result: 46.0%; +0.8% from trail brake depth p95=0.99; +0.2% for high braking decel p95=2.42g; -0.5% for front braking lock proxy p95=0.086; -0.2% for front brake wheel decel asymmetry p95=17.9 m/s^2; Note: measured hydraulic split 70.5% vs recommended 46.3% (delta +24.2%); MC: F/R = 20.6/22.2 mm (ratio 0.93); differs from nominal 1.00 — effective bias may need decrease; Pad: Medium | front-lock evidence shifted hardware seed rearward
supporting.brake_hardware_status: Static brake bias is solved from telemetry; brake target, master cylinders, and pad compound were conservatively seeded from braking evidence. Brake migration stays setup-derived until a real live migration channel is verified.
supporting.diff_reasoning: Base: 27.5 Nm (lat transfer=13751N at peak lat_g=4.03g); +5 Nm for rear power slip p95=0.090; clutch plates=6 | Coast 45 deg (from trail brake depth=0.24); Drive 70 deg (from throttle R^2=0.70) | Lock: coast=62.2% drive=27.2% (preload 7.1% + plates 55.1%)
supporting.tc_reasoning: Baseline: gain=4, slip=3; +1 gain for erratic consistency; +1 slip for rear power slip p95=0.090; +1 gain: TC barely active (0%) but rear slip p95=0.090; +1 slip for high MGU-K torque 359 Nm
supporting.pressure_reasoning: Track temp 24°C → cold adj +1.9 kPa; Front hot 183 kPa → cold 152 kPa; Rear hot 179 kPa → cold 152 kPa

diff_preload_nm: Preload was searched against exit-push, instability, and rear power-slip evidence.
diff_ramp_option_idx: The legal coupled ramp option was searched with extra weight on Sebring long-exit understeer.
diff_clutch_plates: Clutch plate count was searched both toward less lock and more lock based on exit and traction evidence.
